title: National Endowment for the Arts - Grants for Arts Projects
description: Supports artistically excellent projects that celebrate creativity and cultural heritage, including music performances, festivals, and community arts programs.

title: ACFTA - African Culture and Arts Fund
description: Supports African arts organizations, musicians, and cultural institutions working to preserve and promote African music and performing arts across the continent.

title: British Council - Arts Grants for Sub-Saharan Africa
description: Funding for arts and cultural organizations in sub-Saharan Africa to develop creative projects, collaborations, and cultural exchanges with UK partners.

title: Goethe-Institut Nigeria - Cultural Support Grants
description: Supports Nigerian cultural and music organizations with funding for performances, workshops, and cultural exchange programs with German institutions.

title: Ford Foundation - Creativity and Free Expression
description: Supports organizations that use art, culture, and media to promote social justice, with a focus on marginalized communities and creative expression.

title: HEVA Fund - East Africa Creative Economy
description: Provides grants and business support to creative enterprises in East Africa, including music, performing arts, and cultural organizations.

title: New Music USA - Project Grants
description: Supports the creation and performance of new music, funding composers, performers, and music organizations for innovative musical projects.

title: UNESCO International Fund for Cultural Diversity
description: Supports projects that foster the diversity of cultural expressions in developing countries, including music and performing arts organizations.

title: TrustAfrica - Arts and Culture Programme
description: Supports African organizations using arts and culture to drive social change, including music ensembles and community cultural programs.

title: Prince Claus Fund - Cultural and Artistic Grants
description: Supports cultural practitioners and organizations in Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Caribbean with grants for arts and culture projects.
